name: Lebo's Soweto Backpackers
description: Iconic Soweto guesthouse on Vilakazi Street. Award-winning backpackers offering cycling tours, shisa nyama and authentic township experience. The most famous guesthouse in Soweto.

name: Soweto Hotel at Freedom Square
description: Boutique hotel in the heart of Kliptown where the Freedom Charter was signed. Rooftop restaurant with panoramic Soweto views and a rich political heritage.

name: Ubuntu Bed & Breakfast
description: Family-run B&B in Orlando East offering authentic Soweto hospitality. Home-cooked South African breakfast included. Walking distance to Orlando Stadium.

name: Mayfair Soweto Guesthouse
description: Comfortable guesthouse on the border of Mayfair and Soweto. Traditional South Asian-African fusion cuisine reflecting the diverse cultural heritage of the area.

name: Maboneng City Lodge
description: Stylish urban guesthouse in Johannesburg's creative Maboneng precinct. Art galleries, rooftop bars and the famous Market on Main all within walking distance.

name: Eyethu Township Guest House
description: Authentic Soweto guesthouse run by a local family. Traditional pap and vleis dinners on request. Experience real township life in a safe and welcoming environment.

name: Alexandra Township B&B
description: Simple and authentic B&B in Alexandra township, one of Johannesburg's oldest townships. Close to the Sandton business district - township experience meets corporate convenience.

name: Vilakazi Street Guesthouse
description: On the world-famous street that housed two Nobel Peace Prize winners. Comfortable rooms with historical ambience and guided walking tours of the street included.

name: Mandela House Museum
description: Nelson Mandela's former home, now a museum filled with personal memorabilia. Essential visit for understanding South Africa's struggle for freedom.

name: Hector Pieterson Museum
description: Profoundly moving tribute to student activism and the 1976 Soweto Uprising. Documents the bravery of young people who changed the course of South African history.

name: Orlando Towers
description: Iconic painted cooling towers with bungee jumping, base jumping, and Chaf Jozi shisa nyama restaurant. Soweto's most thrilling landmark.

name: Soweto Theatre
description: Premier arts venue hosting theatre, jazz and comedy performances. Monthly arts and crafts fair. Architecturally stunning building worth visiting on its own.

name: Regina Mundi Catholic Church
description: Soweto's largest church, used as a refuge and meeting point during the 1976 uprising. Bullet holes still visible. An emotionally powerful heritage site.

name: Market on Main
description: Johannesburg's most celebrated Sunday market in the creative Maboneng precinct. Street food, art, vintage clothing, live music and local designers every Sunday.

name: Credo Mutwa Cultural Village
description: Traditional African cultural heritage village showcasing San and African mythology, sculptures and artifacts created by Vusamazulu Credo Mutwa, Africa's greatest living storyteller.

name: Vicky's B&B
description: Award-winning B&B run by Vicky who has welcomed visitors from over 50 countries. Authentic Khayelitsha experience with home-cooked breakfast and guided community walks.

name: Majoro's Khayelitsha Guesthouse
description: Welcoming township guesthouse in Khayelitsha. Majoro hosts cooking classes, shebeen tours and community garden visits. Rated one of Cape Town's most unique stays.

name: Langa Township Guesthouse
description: Stay in Cape Town's oldest township, founded in 1927. Your host will take you on a walking tour of Langa's historic streets, shebeens and the famous Pass Museum.

name: Gugulethu Ubuntu Lodge
description: Cultural lodge in the heart of Gugulethu offering immersive township experiences. Mzoli's famous shisa nyama is a short walk away. Jazz evenings and art tours arranged.

name: Bo-Kaap Heritage House
description: Stay in one of Cape Town's iconic cobblestone street houses in the historic Bo-Kaap (Cape Malay Quarter). Traditional Cape Malay cuisine and mountain views.

name: Khayelitsha Wellness Lodge
description: Community-owned lodge combining township hospitality with wellness. Traditional healing, herbal gardens and Xhosa cultural experiences. Profits support local youth programmes.

name: District Six Guesthouse
description: Stay adjacent to the historic District Six, where 60,000 people were forcibly removed during apartheid. The District Six Museum is a 5-minute walk from your door.

name: 18 Gangster Museum
description: Africa's first living museum about gangsterism, curated by former offenders. An immersive, powerful experience educating youth about the pitfalls of gangsterism and drugs.

name: Langa Pass Museum
description: Documents the powerful political history of the pass laws. Langa was home to a 50,000-strong protest march against apartheid pass laws. Essential heritage site.

name: Guga S'thebe Culture Centre
description: Vibrant community arts centre named after the traditional Xhosa serving platter. Signifies Ubuntu as a meeting place for creative exchange, performances and exhibitions.

name: Mzoli's Place
description: Cape Town's most famous township butchery and tavern. Choose your meat, Mzoli's team grills it on the braai while you enjoy music and community atmosphere.

name: Makukhanye Art Room
description: Vibrant community art space showcasing township artists and youth creative development. Colourful murals, workshops and exhibitions celebrating Khayelitsha's creative spirit.

name: Khayelitsha Wines
description: Unique township winery breaking stereotypes about wine culture in South Africa. Wine tastings in a township setting - an experience unlike anywhere else in the world.

name: Siki's Coffee
description: Township coffee shop celebrating local entrepreneurship and youth empowerment. Fresh brews, local pastries and great conversations in the heart of Khayelitsha.

name: District Six Museum
description: World-renowned museum documenting the forced removal of 60,000 inhabitants during the 1970s. One of Cape Town's most important and moving heritage sites.

name: Inanda Heritage Lodge
description: Cultural lodge on the Inanda Heritage Route. Walk in the footsteps of Gandhi, Mandela and John Dube. Guided heritage tours, traditional Zulu meals and drumming evenings.

name: Umlazi Township Guesthouse
description: Comfortable guesthouse in Umlazi, the second largest township in South Africa. Your host arranges traditional Zulu dance performances and shisanyama experiences.

name: KwaMashu Community Guesthouse
description: Stay in KwaMashu, Durban's oldest township, renowned for its vibrant performance arts scene. Hip-hop, pantsula dancing and kwaito music form the soundtrack of daily life.

name: Valley of 1000 Hills Lodge
description: Cultural lodge in the breathtaking Valley of a Thousand Hills. Traditional Zulu village visits, cultural performances, zip-lining and spectacular views of the valley.

name: eNanda Adventure Camp
description: Camping on the water's edge at Inanda Dam. Hiking to Mzinyathi Falls, canoeing, fishing and birdwatching with deep historical heritage around every corner.

name: Clermont Ubuntu B&B
description: Budget-friendly B&B in Clermont township. Authentic Zulu hospitality, home-cooked meals and a genuine community experience within easy reach of Durban city centre.

name: Durban Indian Quarter Guesthouse
description: Stay in the heart of Durban's vibrant Indian Quarter, home to the largest Indian population outside India. Victoria Street Market, curry houses and mosques steps away.

name: Inanda Heritage Route
description: Historic trail starting at Gandhi's Phoenix Settlement, passing Inanda Seminary, Ohlange Institute (where Mandela voted in 1994) and the Shembe Church at Ebuhleni.

name: Shembe Church (Nazareth Baptist Church)
description: Declared a world heritage site. The Shembe Church is a remarkable blend of Zulu tradition and Christian faith with elaborate rituals and vibrant gospel culture.

name: Mxenge Museum
description: Former home of struggle icons Advocate Griffiths and Victoria Mxenge, both killed fighting apartheid. A powerful reminder of the price of freedom.

name: Max's Lifestyle Village
description: Umlazi's most celebrated restaurant. Traditional kasi culture in upmarket, safe surroundings. People from all walks of life come for the food, music and township atmosphere.

name: Victoria Street Market
description: Durban's oldest and most vibrant market selling spices, traditional medicine, curios, clothing and fresh produce. The beating heart of Durban's Indian and Zulu trading culture.

name: KwaMashu Performance Arts Centre
description: Hub of KwaMashu's world-renowned performance arts scene. Hip-hop, pantsula, gumboot dancing and kwaito performances celebrating the township's unique cultural identity.

name: PheZulu Safari Park
address: Old Main Road, Valley of 1000 Hills
description: Traditional Zulu cultural village and wildlife experience in the Valley of a Thousand Hills. Zulu dancing, crocodiles, snakes and stunning valley views all in one.

name: Gandhi Settlement Phoenix
description: Where Mahatma Gandhi established his passive resistance movement in South Africa in 1904. The Phoenix Settlement shaped Gandhi's philosophy of non-violent resistance.
